title: Romantic Chinese Pork Char-Siu
description: A sweet and savory Chinese dish featuring marinated pork, 5-spice powder, and a caramelized glaze. This dish is perfect for special occasions or romantic dates.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together five-spice powder, brown sugar, rice vinegar, and soy sauce to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and bake the pork for 20 minutes.
While the pork is baking, prepare the glaze by whisking together honey and red food coloring (if using).
After 20 minutes, brush the glaze over the pork and continue baking for another 10-15 minutes, or until caramelized.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the char siu with scallions on top (if using).
To serve, place the sliced pork on a platter or individual plates and garnish with additional scallions and red food coloring (if using).
